duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VoteWeighting.removeNominee fails to subtract the removed nominee's slope from pointsSum[nextTime].slope, causing the global weight sum to decay at an incorrect rate and distorting all nomineeRelativeWeight calculations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the identical root cause: removeNominee updates pointsSum[nextTime].bias but not pointsSum[nextTime].slope. TECH-H001 provides the most comprehensive technical analysis referencing all affected downstream functions (_getSum, _nomineeRelativeWeight, calculateStakingIncentives). ECON-H012 adds an interesting angle about uninitialized slope becoming 0, but this is a consequence of the same bug.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VoteWeighting.removeNominee does not clean up future changesSum/changesWeight entries for voters who directed votes to the removed nominee, causing ongoing slope distortion at lock expiration times
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same stale changesSum/changesWeight entries persisting after nominee removal due to reliance on voluntary revokeRemovedNomineeVotingPower calls. TECH-H007 provides the most detailed technical mechanism description and identifies the game-theoretic issue of users having no incentive to clean up.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EthereumDepositProcessor._deposit hard-reverts with TargetEmissionsZero if any single target in a batch has zero emissions limit, blocking all L1 staking targets in the same batch — unlike L2 dispensers which gracefully withhold and continue
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine threats describe the same underlying defect: EthereumDepositProcessor._deposit uses a hard revert on zero limitAmount instead of graceful handling. ECON-H007 and OPS2-H010 add permissionless nominee/factory creation as the attack vector, but the exploited vulnerability is the same L1 hard revert. TECH-H005 provides the most focused and precise technical analysis with clear code line references and explicit comparison to L2 behavior.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DefaultTargetDispenserL2.redeem() does not re-verify staking target validity via stakingFactory.verifyInstanceAndGetEmissionsAmount, allowing queued deposits to flow to targets that became invalid after queuing (TOCTOU)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical TOCTOU vulnerability: redeem() checks only the queue hash existence without re-calling verifyInstanceAndGetEmissionsAmount. Note: the TECH2-H003 referenced here is the first instance with title 'Queued Staking Deposits Bypass Emissions Limit Verification on Redemption'. TECH-H003 provides the clearest description of the vulnerability mechanism and affected code paths.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking() checks msg.sender against dispenser but error message incorrectly reports depository address: revert ManagerOnly(msg.sender, depository)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the identical wrong-variable-in-error-message bug. TECH-H004 is the most concise and precise with exact line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives() reverts with ClaimIncentivesFailed when both reward and topUp are zero because the success variable is never set to true in the zero-incentive path
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the identical uninitialized success boolean causing unnecessary revert on zero claims. Note: the TECH2-H004 referenced here is the second instance with title 'Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives() can be permanently DoSed'. TECH-H009 provides the clearest technical description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: StakingFactory.setInstanceStatus() restricts enable/disable to original deployer only, with no factory owner override, preventing emergency disablement of malicious instances
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the identical access control gap where factory owner lacks instance-level override. TECH-H010 provides the most comprehensive analysis including the permissionless createStakingInstance context.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Tokenomics._trackServiceDonations truncates donation amounts via uint96(amounts[i] / numServiceUnits), losing the remainder which is still counted in totalDonationsETH but never distributed to units
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the identical integer division truncation causing undistributed ETH. TECH-H012 provides the most detailed analysis of how the discrepancy propagates through the accounting system.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: StakingBase checkpoint reward rounding dust is systematically assigned to the first eligible service (index 0) rather than distributed fairly
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical rounding dust allocation to eligibleServiceIds[0]. ECON-H008 provides better context about the loop structure and proportional scaling path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Tokenomics._finalizeIncentivesForUnitId casts accumulated uint256 totalIncentives to uint96 without overflow checking, silently truncating large accumulated rewards/topUps
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the identical unsafe uint96 downcast in _finalizeIncentivesForUnitId. TECH-H006 provides the most detailed analysis of the accumulation formula that could overflow.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Tokenomics.initializeTokenomics() is external with only owner==address(0) guard, enabling front-running takeover if deployed without atomic initialization
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical permissionless initialization vulnerability on the Tokenomics contract. TECH-H013 provides a more comprehensive impact analysis including downstream effects on managers and PROXY_TOKENOMICS slot.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: StakingToken/StakingNativeToken initialize() functions are external with only single-init guard, enabling attacker initialization if deployed outside StakingFactory's atomic workflow
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical permissionless initialization vulnerability on staking implementations. TECH-H014 provides broader context about downstream verification configuration dependencies.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Dispenser does not validate mapChainIdDepositProcessors[chainId] is non-zero before transferring OLAS and calling sendMessage, risking token burns or DoS when chain mapping is unset
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical missing zero-address validation for deposit processor lookups. TECH-H015 provides more context about the intentional zero-setting behavior documented in setDepositProcessorChainIds.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: StakingBase.unstake() uses a stale array index from before checkpoint() evictions, potentially causing out-of-bounds access or incorrect element removal in setServiceIds
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical stale-index-after-eviction vulnerability in unstake(). ECON-H013 provides a more specific analysis of the out-of-bounds consequence and includes attack economics showing minimal capital needed.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Tokenomics.checkpoint() effectiveBond adjustment only handles the case where actual maxBond exceeds estimate, missing the downward adjustment when inflation drops at year boundaries
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical asymmetric effectiveBond adjustment bug at year boundaries. TECH2-H002 provides clearer identification of the missing else branch and the pattern of asymmetric state adjustment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VoteWeighting._getSum()/_getWeight() loop bounded by MAX_NUM_WEEKS (53) fails to advance timeSum/timeWeight if not checkpointed for >53 weeks, causing permanent state stagnation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical checkpoint stagnation bug when exceeding MAX_NUM_WEEKS. Note: the TECH2-H006 referenced here is the first instance with title 'VoteWeighting Checkpoint Stagnation if Missed for Over 53 Weeks'. TECH2-H006 provides the most precise technical analysis of why timeSum is never updated when the loop exhausts.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless calls to VoteWeighting.checkpointNominee() and Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives() allow anyone to force-write gauge weight state, potentially influencing timing of weight updates
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same concern about permissionless state-modifying checkpoint calls. OPS-H013 covers the broader scope (direct VoteWeighting calls and downstream effects) while OPS2-H005 focuses on the indirect path through Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives. Same underlying issue.
